summary: Feature request to support SkiaSharp as a Blazor Extension enabling both client-side WASM and server-side Blazor rendering. Client-side WASM was shipped via SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor (PR #1811, Oct 2021), but server-side Blazor support remains unimplemented due to the architecture requiring in-browser JS interop and native WASM binaries.

repoLinks.description: Example of HTML5 Canvas Blazor Extension supporting both client and server rendering
Maintainer prototype using SKPicture serialization over SignalR for server-to-client rendering
Maintainer sample for client-side Blazor WebAssembly with SkiaSharp

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Client-side WASM was shipped, but server-side Blazor rendering is still not supported. The SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package has [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] and JSModuleInterop throws NotSupportedException for non-WASM runtimes.

analysis.summary: The original request had two parts: (1) client-side Blazor WASM support and (2) server-side Blazor support via a 'Blazor Extension' pattern (marshaling draw calls over SignalR). Part 1 was fully addressed by PR #1811 which shipped SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor. Part 2 remains unimplemented. The current architecture requires in-browser execution: SKCanvasView uses [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")], JSModuleInterop requires IJSInProcessRuntime (pre-.NET 7) or JSHost.ImportAsync (.NET 7+), and rendering depends on HTML Canvas and requestAnimationFrame. Server-side Blazor would need a fundamentally different approach, as demonstrated by mattleibow's BlazorSkiaSharp prototype using SKPicture serialization.
analysis.rationale: Classified as feature-request because the reporter explicitly asks for new functionality (server-side Blazor Extension support). The client-side WASM part was delivered, but the distinguishing 'Extension' pattern — allowing the same SkiaSharp API to work transparently on both client and server — was never implemented. The issue remains open and the last comment (#48, May 2022) specifically asks about server-side support timeline. High community interest with 19 reactions and 48 comments supports keeping this open.

keySignals.text: the feature request is once SkiaSharp can run in Client Blazor, please support SkiaSharp as a Blazor Extension
keySignals.interpretation: The core ask is the 'Extension' pattern supporting both client and server deployment models.

keySignals.text: I have merged #1811 and will release a public preview of the new client-side, wasm SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package
keySignals.interpretation: Client-side WASM was shipped, addressing half the request.

keySignals.text: SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package still works in Blazor WebAssembly apps only... Is there a timeframe for when the functionality will be supported on Server-side Blazor?
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms server-side Blazor is still not supported and there is ongoing demand.

keySignals.text: Think of SKPicture as a binary serialization of the final drawing commands that are going to be sent to the client
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer explored SKPicture-based approach for server-to-client rendering, providing a feasible architecture.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKCanvasView has [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] — explicitly limited to browser/WASM platform. No server-side rendering path exists.
JSModuleInterop requires browser runtime: on pre-.NET 7, throws NotSupportedException if not IJSInProcessRuntime; on .NET 7+ uses JSHost.ImportAsync which requires browser. No server-side fallback.
SKHtmlCanvasInterop uses [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] and relies on HTML Canvas DOM APIs (putImageData, requestAnimationFrame) — inherently client-side.
SupportedPlatform is limited to 'browser' only. Package depends on SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly — no server native assets.

nextQuestions: Is the SKPicture serialization approach (BlazorSkiaSharp prototype) viable for productionization?
Would Blazor's interactive render modes in .NET 8+ change the server-side architecture requirements?
Should server-side support live in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor or a separate package (e.g., SkiaSharp.Extended)?

resolution.hypothesis: Server-side Blazor support would require a new rendering architecture that serializes drawing commands (e.g., SKPicture) on the server and transmits them to a client-side renderer over SignalR, rather than executing native drawing APIs directly.

proposals.title: Server-side rendering to static images
proposals.description: Use SkiaSharp on the server to render to SKImage/SKBitmap, encode as PNG/JPEG, and serve via tags or data URIs. This works today without any framework changes but doesn't provide interactive canvas behavior.

proposals.title: SKPicture serialization over SignalR
proposals.description: Build on mattleibow's BlazorSkiaSharp prototype: serialize drawing commands as SKPicture on server, transmit via SignalR to client-side WASM SkiaSharp for rendering. Provides interactive canvas with server-side business logic.

proposals.title: Use SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor with interactive WASM render mode
proposals.description: In .NET 8+ Blazor apps with interactive WebAssembly render mode, the existing SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package works for components that render client-side. Business logic can still run on the server with only the rendering component in WASM.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor with interactive WASM render mode
resolution.recommendedReason: .NET 8+ Blazor's interactive render modes allow mixing server and client components, which partially addresses the original request pattern without requiring a new architecture.

actionability.reason: Client-side WASM was delivered but the core feature request (server-side Blazor Extension pattern) remains unimplemented. High community interest (19 reactions, 48 comments) and the last comment explicitly asks about server-side support.

actions.description: Apply feature-request, Blazor, and WASM labels
Acknowledge partial progress, clarify remaining gap, and suggest .NET 8+ interactive render mode as interim solution
actions.comment: Thanks for this feature request and the ongoing discussion. The client-side Blazor WASM support was shipped in `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` (merged in #1811). The remaining gap is server-side Blazor support, where SkiaSharp draw calls on the server would be marshaled to the client for rendering. For server-side scenarios today, there are two approaches: 1. **Static image rendering** — Use SkiaSharp on the server to render to PNG/JPEG and serve via `` tags. This works today but doesn't provide interactive canvas behavior. 2. **Interactive WASM render mode (.NET 8+)** — With Blazor's interactive WebAssembly render mode, you can keep business logic on the server while the `SKCanvasView` component renders client-side in WASM. This partially addresses the original "Extension" pattern. The full server-side rendering architecture (serializing draw commands over SignalR) explored in the [BlazorSkiaSharp prototype](https://github.com/mattleibow/BlazorSkiaSharp) remains a potential future direction but would require significant work.

actions.description: Link to the Blazor WASM views PR that addressed client-side support
Link to Mobile Blazor Bindings feature request
